stimulus: The Gini coefficient, a widely cited measure of inequality in the distribution of income across a population, ranges from a score of zero to one. Compared with that of a highly unequal economy—that is, an economy in which wealth is concentrated in a tiny share of households—______blank
stem: Which choice completes the text so that it conforms to the conventions of Standard English?

answerOptions.content: a more egalitarian economy will register a much lower Gini coefficient.
when an economy is more egalitarian, its Gini coefficient will be much lower.
an economy that is more egalitarian will produce a much lower Gini coefficient.
the Gini coefficient of a more egalitarian economy will be much lower.

rationale:
Choice D is the best answer. The convention being tested is subject-modifier placement. This choice makes the noun phrase “the Gini coefficient of a more egalitarian economy” the subject of the sentence and places it immediately after the modifying phrase “Compared with that of a highly unequal economy.” In doing so, this choice clearly establishes that the Gini coefficient of a more egalitarian economy—and not another noun in the sentence—is being compared with the Gini coefficient of a highly unequal economy.

Choice A is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of “a more egalitarian economy” immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that an economy is being compared with a Gini coefficient. Choice B is incorrect because it results in a confusing, illogical sentence. The placement of the modifier “when an economy is more egalitarian” after “a highly unequal economy” is contradictory, and “its” is ambiguous. Choice C is incorrect because it results in a dangling modifier. The placement of “an economy that is more egalitarian” immediately after the modifying phrase illogically suggests that an economy is being compared with a Gini coefficient.
